displayTitle: The Coil Shot: Flux Along, Flaws Across

beats.narration: Bend that current-carrying wire into a coil and the field changes: flux now runs straight down the axis. That's longitudinal magnetization.

payload.title: THE COIL SHOT
payload.sub: Bend the wire into a coil — the field turns longitudinal.

beats.narration: Slide steel inside. Hundreds of times more permeable than air, the part pulls flux in to run along its length. No contact, no burns.

payload.heading: Induction pulls flux into the part
payload.note: FLUX TAKES THE STEEL PATH · NO CURRENT IN PART

beats.narration: Longitudinal flux must exit the ends and return through air, forming poles — so demagnetize after. Fuzzy end buildup is pole leakage, not a defect.

chips.t: North and south poles at the ends

beats.narration: Flux runs along the part and crosses flaws around or across it: transverse cracks, circumferential cracks at shoulders and threads. Axial seams? Circular shot's job.
So the rule: flux along, flaws across. Pair it with a circular shot and you've covered every orientation.
